name: Meghalaya (मेघालय / 'Abode of Clouds')
summary: Meghalaya (मेघालय in Hindi, meaning 'Abode of Clouds') is a North-eastern Indian Khasi-Jaintia-Garo Hills mixed common-law/Khasi-customary-law/Jaintia-customary-law/Garo-customary-law constituent state of the Indian Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian state operating Constitution Sixth Schedule tribal-areas framework as the primary governance framework with three Autonomous District Councils (Khasi Hills Autonomous District Council, Jaintia Hills Autonomous District Council, Garo Hills Autonomous District Council) operating as the most comprehensive Indian sub-state tribal-autonomy framework, as the only Indian state with matrilineal-society constitutional framework where Khasi, Jaintia, and Garo customary law explicitly recognise matrilineal inheritance and family-property frameworks (where property and clan-membership pass through the maternal line — only modern Indian state with constitutionally-protected matrilineal customary law), and as the central jurisdiction of the most distinctive Indian state-level Christian-majority population framework (~75% Christian, predominantly Roman Catholic and Presbyterian, established through 19th-century Welsh Calvinist Methodist Mission). Meghalaya was carved out of Assam as the 21st state of India on 21 January 1972 under the North-Eastern Areas (Reorganisation) Act 1971. Meghalaya comprises ~22,429 km² with a population of ~3 million. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Indian common-law + Khasi/Jaintia/Garo matrilineal-customary-law framework with customary law applied via Autonomous District Council customary courts under Sixth Schedule framework. Parental authority and child custody operate under matrilineal customary law for Khasi/Jaintia/Garo persons subject to Indian general framework. The Meghalaya High Court (established 23 March 2013, separating from Gauhati High Court Shillong Bench) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Meghalaya is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Meghalaya is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of India Sixth Schedule (Meghalaya Autonomous District Councils)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution Sixth Schedule
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Sixth Schedule establishing tribal-areas autonomous-district-council framework applicable in Meghalaya — Khasi Hills Autonomous District Council + Jaintia Hills Autonomous District Council + Garo Hills Autonomous District Council.

statutory_framework.title: North-Eastern Areas (Reorganisation) Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act of 1971 establishing the State of Meghalaya effective 21 January 1972 — carving out Meghalaya from Assam.

statutory_framework.citation: Khasi Hills Autonomous District (Khasi Social Custom of Lineage) Act 1997
statutory_framework.title: Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Khasi Hills Autonomous District Council Act establishing matrilineal lineage framework for Khasi persons — constitutionally-protected matrilineal customary law.

apex_courts.name: Meghalaya High Court
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Meghalaya — established 23 March 2013.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Meghalaya.

apex_courts.name: Khasi/Jaintia/Garo Hills Autonomous District Councils (customary courts)
apex_courts.role: Autonomous District Council customary courts operating under Sixth Schedule framework.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Meghalaya.

anonymisation_convention: Meghalaya family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Yandabo + British-Khasi-Jaintia-Hills-administration framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Yandabo 24 February 1826 ending First Anglo-Burmese War — substantively distinctive North-eastern Indian British-Khasi-Jaintia-Hills-administration framework. Foundational pre-1841-Welsh-Calvinist-Mission framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Christianisation-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Welsh Calvinist Methodist Mission + Christianisation framework
key_developments.description: Welsh Calvinist Methodist Mission to Khasi and Jaintia Hills commenced 22 June 1841 (Thomas Jones Sr arrival) — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-major-Indian-sub-state-Christian-population-via-Welsh-Calvinist-Methodist-Mission framework. Foundational substantive Christianisation framework leading to ~75% Christian-majority population persisting through subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Garo Hills administration + 1860-Garo-Hills-Act framework
key_developments.description: Garo Hills Act 1872 (and earlier 1860 Garo Hills regulation) substantively reshaping British-Garo-Hills-administration framework — substantively distinctive North-eastern Indian Garo-tribal-administration framework. Foundational pre-1950-Sixth-Schedule framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sixth Schedule applicable to Khasi-Jaintia-Garo Hills + Indian Constitution framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of India Sixth Schedule effective 26 January 1950 applicable to Khasi, Jaintia, and Garo Hills (then part of Assam) — substantively distinctive globally Indian-constitutional-tribal-areas-autonomous-district-council framework establishing Khasi/Jaintia/Garo autonomous-district-council framework. Foundational substantive Sixth-Schedule framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Meghalaya State established + 21st-state + Sixth-Schedule-continuation framework
key_developments.description: Meghalaya formed as 21st state of India 21 January 1972 under North-Eastern Areas (Reorganisation) Act 1971 — substantively distinctive globally Sixth-Schedule-primary-governance-framework-as-state framework. Substantive Sixth Schedule framework continued affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: India ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + customary-law-co-applicability framework
key_developments.description: India ratified UNCRC 11 December 1992 with applicability to Meghalaya — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Indian-derivative-common-law framework + Khasi/Jaintia/Garo matrilineal-customary-law co-applicability. Foundational substantive framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act + constitutionally-protected-matrilineal-customary-law framework
key_developments.description: Khasi Hills Autonomous District (Khasi Social Custom of Lineage) Act 1997 enacted — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-Indian-state-with-constitutionally-protected-matrilineal-customary-law framework. Substantive matrilineal-lineage framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation for Khasi persons.

key_developments.title: Meghalaya High Court established + state-level-judicial-autonomy framework
key_developments.description: Meghalaya High Court established 23 March 2013 separating from Gauhati High Court Shillong Bench under North-Eastern Areas (Reorganisation) and Other Related Laws (Amendment) Act 2012 — substantively distinctive North-eastern Indian state-level-judicial-autonomy framework. Substantive judicial framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Meghalaya Residents Safety and Security Act 2016 + 2020-Amendment + inner-line-permit-equivalent framework
key_developments.description: Meghalaya Residents Safety and Security Act 2016 enacted + 2020 Amendments — substantively distinctive North-eastern Indian inner-line-permit-equivalent framework regulating outsider entry. Substantive Khasi/Jaintia/Garo identity-preservation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Conrad Sangma NPP Government + KSU-+-FKJGP-+-Khasi-Jaintia-identity-organisations framework
key_developments.description: Conrad Sangma (NPP) Chief Minister framework continuing through 2023 following March 2023 Assembly elections with Khasi Students' Union (KSU) + Federation of Khasi, Jaintia, and Garo People (FKJGP) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Sixth Schedule + Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act 1997 framework. Continuing inner-line-permit-equivalent Meghalaya Residents Safety and Security Act 2016/2020 framework substantively significant institutional context. Meghalaya High Court continuing matrilineal-customary-law jurisprudence framework. Substantively significant North-eastern Indian Khasi-Jaintia-Garo-Hills institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Meghalaya High Court + Supreme Court + Autonomous District Council customary courts — best-interests-of-the-child + matrilineal-customary-law substantive register + continuing-Sixth-Schedule framework
key_developments.description: Meghalaya High Court, Supreme Court of India, and Khasi/Jaintia/Garo Hills Autonomous District Council customary courts continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child + matrilineal-customary-law jurisprudence under Sixth Schedule + Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act 1997 framework in custody disputes within continuing Sixth-Schedule autonomous-district-council framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Meghalaya operates a mixed federal Indian common-law + Khasi/Jaintia/Garo matrilineal-customary-law framework — places Meghalaya in the North-eastern Indian Indigenous-tribal-customary-law-protected cluster.

Only Indian state operating Constitution Sixth Schedule tribal-areas framework as the primary governance framework with three Autonomous District Councils is structurally distinctive globally — most comprehensive Indian sub-state tribal-autonomy framework.

Only Indian state with matrilineal-society constitutional framework where Khasi, Jaintia, and Garo customary law explicitly recognise matrilineal inheritance and family-property frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Indian state with constitutionally-protected matrilineal customary law.
Central jurisdiction of most distinctive Indian state-level Christian-majority population framework (~75% Christian) is structurally distinctive within Indian state cluster.

Welsh Calvinist Methodist Mission 1841+ Christianisation framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern major Indian sub-state Christian population established via Welsh Calvinist Methodist mission framework.

Khasi Hills + Jaintia Hills + Garo Hills three-Autonomous-District-Council framework is structurally distinctive globally — only Indian state with three constitutionally-recognised tribal Autonomous District Councils operating under Sixth Schedule.
Meghalaya High Court 2013 establishment is structurally distinctive within Indian state High Court establishment cluster.
editorial_notes: Meghalaya jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Treaty-of-Yandabo-1826-to-Meghalaya-Residents-Safety-and-Security-Act trajectory: 1826-Treaty-of-Yandabo-+-British-administration + 1841-Welsh-Calvinist-Methodist-Mission-+-Christianisation + 1872-Garo-Hills-Act-+-Garo-tribal-administration + 1950-Sixth-Schedule-+-Indian-Constitution + 1972-Meghalaya-State-established + 1992-India-UNCRC-ratification-+-customary-law-co-applicability + 1997-Khasi-Hills-Matrilineal-Lineage-Act + 2013-Meghalaya-High-Court-established + 2020-Meghalaya-Residents-Safety-and-Security-Act-Amendment + 2024-Meghalaya-HC-+-SC-+-ADC-customary-courts-matrilineal.

Mixed federal Indian common-law/Khasi/Jaintia/Garo matrilineal-customary-law North-eastern Indian Khasi-Jaintia-Garo Hills Indian-state (Indian Constitution Sixth Schedule + Khasi Hills + Jaintia Hills + Garo Hills Autonomous District Councils + North-Eastern Areas (Reorganisation) Act 1971 + Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act 1997 + Welsh Calvinist Methodist Mission Christianisation framework + Meghalaya High Court 2013 + Indian non-Hague-1980). Only Indian state operating Constitution Sixth Schedule tribal-areas framework as primary governance with three Autonomous District Councils globally + only Indian state with matrilineal-society constitutional framework + most distinctive Indian state-level Christian-majority population (~75%) + only modern major Indian sub-state Christian population via Welsh Calvinist Methodist mission framework.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child + matrilineal-customary-law analysis under Sixth Schedule + Khasi Hills Matrilineal Lineage Act 1997 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-eastern-Indian + Khasi-Jaintia-Garo-Hills + mixed-Indian-common-law/Khasi/Jaintia/Garo-matrilineal-customary-law + Indian-state-cluster + Constitution-Sixth-Schedule-primary-governance-framework-globally-distinctive + matrilineal-customary-law-constitutional-framework-globally-distinctive + three-Autonomous-District-Councils-globally-distinctive + ~75-percent-Christian-majority-most-distinctive-Indian-state + Welsh-Calvinist-Methodist-Mission-1841-Thomas-Jones-globally-distinctive + Treaty-of-Yandabo-1826-First-Anglo-Burmese-War + Garo-Hills-Act-1872 + Khasi-Hills-Matrilineal-Lineage-Act-1997 + Meghalaya-High-Court-2013 + Meghalaya-Residents-Safety-and-Security-Act-2016-2020-inner-line-permit-equivalent + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
